what_it_does: Validates, persists, and registers declared LLM actions (chain/graph configurations) in a JSON-backed ActionRegistry, and projects a read-only coherence summary from a store.
observations: Single validation gate build_action for all action declarations; enforces registry-is-truth for models and optional live role/rule/check/panel/jury registries.
Supports seven step ops: generate, embed, similarity, retrieve, detect, reduce, check.
Special role-less step kinds: rule-reduce, retrieve, check, and panel.
Jury roles must be used with kind='jury'; non-jury generate steps cannot use jury roles.
unit_ctx requires address templates containing a '{' placeholder; shared inputs require exactly one of address/text/corpus_query.
ActionRegistry reloads from disk when the file mtime changes and writes atomically via tmp+replace.
save_calibration attaches a winning calibrated config to an existing action.
build_coherence_info is a pure read projection (burn-down, finding kinds, dispositions, open findings).
standouts: N3 save/run parity: optional caller-supplied registries make validation sufficient for runnable actions.
Atomic persistence and mtime-based hot-reload in a simple JSON registry.
Teaching-oriented fail-loud error messages embedded in validation.
Jury semantics guard against silently dropping multi-draw verdict roles.
conventions: Registry-is-truth: models, roles, panels, checks, and rules are referenced by registered ids rather than literals.
Additive/fail-loud: returns {"ok": False, "error": ...} instead of raising for validation failures.
Pure projection for read-face (build_coherence_info) without side effects.
Optional resolver injection for rule/check/panel validation.
concerns: unit_ctx only checks that template strings contain '{', not that placeholders match actual unit fields.
fan_field is validated as a non-empty string but not verified against the prior step's output schema.
max_tokens rejects bool because bool is a subclass of int; the check is correct but the error wording may confuse.
_reload_if_stale opens the file inline without an explicit close reference.
No concurrency locking around _flush and concurrent reads.
notes: This is the saving-side gate for LLM actions: a strict, teaching validator plus a JSON-backed registry with hot-reload and calibration attachment.
questions: What is the exact schema/contract for output_schema and schema_ver?
How is the models set populated so it includes both chat and embedding models?
What does panel_resolver return and what is a verdict_panels row structure?
What is the structure of a calibration dict and which component consumes it?
How does the runner interpret unit_ctx templates and shared specs?
What is the relationship between action steps and cognition.run_items/run_reduce/run_graph?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it declares, validates, saves, registers actions and projects coherence info, without implementing the runner.
intention_signals: The file tries to make action declarations safe to save and later run by centralizing validation through one door, keeping all model/role/rule references live-registry-backed, and providing durable, reloadable storage plus a read-only coherence dashboard.
novelty: The explicit save/run parity gate (N3) that optionally enforces runner requirements at build time, and the mtime-aware hot-reload in a simple JSON registry.
contribution: A reusable validator for action declarations, a persistent JSON action registry with atomic writes and hot-reload, and a pure projection of coherence status for read-only consumers.
summary_for_embedding: coherence_actions.py implements the saving-side action lifecycle: a single validation gate for declared LLM chain/graph actions, a JSON-backed ActionRegistry with atomic persistence and mtime-based reload, and a read-only coherence-info projector.
intention_for_embedding: To ensure declared actions are registry-valid, persistable, reloadable, and runnable before they reach the execution engine, while exposing coherence status without mutating state.

symbols.does: Validates an action declaration against model and optional live registries, returning an action dict or a teaching error.
symbols.description: build_action is the single validation door for action declarations. It checks name and steps, enforces that any model is in the live model registry, validates roles, reduce rules, jury semantics, panel/check references, unit_ctx templates, fan_field, max_tokens, and shared-input shape. When optional role/rule/check/panel resolvers are supplied it also enforces runner-parity requirements so ok=True implies runnable.

symbols.does: JSON-backed persistent store for validated actions with hot-reload and atomic writes.
symbols.description: ActionRegistry loads saved actions from a JSON file, keyed by name, and reloads automatically when the file mtime changes. It supports saving actions, attaching calibration metadata, and listing or retrieving actions by name. Writes are atomic via a temporary file and os.replace.

symbols.does: Initializes the registry for a file path and performs an initial load.
symbols.description: Stores the persistence path, initializes an empty action map and mtime sentinel, then calls _reload_if_stale to populate actions from disk if the file exists.

symbols.does: Reloads the action map from disk when the file modification time changes.
symbols.description: Stats the registry file on every read; if the mtime differs from the cached value, it reparses the JSON actions list into a name-keyed dict. This lets long-lived processes see out-of-process saves without polling.

symbols.does: Atomically persists the current action map to the registry file.
symbols.description: Creates parent directories if needed, writes the full actions list to a temporary file, then renames it over the target path with os.replace to make the update atomic.

symbols.does: Stores or updates an action in memory and flushes it to disk.
symbols.description: Inserts the action into the internal map keyed by its name and calls _flush to persist the registry immediately.

symbols.does: Attaches a calibration dict to a named action and persists.
symbols.description: Looks up the action by name, sets its calibration field to the supplied dict, and flushes the registry. Raises KeyError if the action does not exist.

symbols.does: Retrieves a single action by name, reloading first if the file changed.
symbols.description: Calls _reload_if_stale to ensure freshness, then returns the action dict or None if the name is absent.

symbols.does: Returns a list of all stored actions, reloading first if the file changed.
symbols.description: Calls _reload_if_stale and returns a list copy of the action values.

symbols.does: Projects a read-only coherence summary from a store.
symbols.description: Computes a burn-down via coherence_detect.burn_down, derives finding kinds from real findings, lists valid dispositions, and returns open findings. It writes nothing, serving as the read-face for coherence status.
